title: Password Generator
description: Free secure password generator. Create strong random passwords using uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols. Runs entirely in your browser.
label: Generated Password
placeholder: Click Generate to create a password

settings.excludeAmbiguous: Exclude ambiguous characters (0, O, l, I)

toast.noCharset: Please enable at least one character type

howToUse.label: Set length
howToUse.description: Use the slider or input to choose a password length between 4 and 128 characters.

howToUse.label: Choose character types
howToUse.description: Toggle uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols as needed.

howToUse.label: Generate
howToUse.description: Click "Generate" to create a cryptographically secure random password.

howToUse.label: Copy
howToUse.description: Click "Copy" to copy the password to your clipboard.

faq.question: Is the generated password truly random?
faq.answer: Yes. Passwords are generated using the Web Crypto API (crypto.getRandomValues()), which produces cryptographically secure random numbers. No Math.random() is used.

faq.question: Is my password sent to a server?
faq.answer: No. Everything runs in your browser. No data is transmitted to any server, and no passwords are stored anywhere.

faq.question: How long should my password be?
faq.answer: At minimum 12 characters for personal accounts, 16+ for financial or sensitive accounts. Longer is always better — 20+ characters with mixed character types is recommended.

faq.question: What does entropy mean?
faq.answer: Entropy (measured in bits) represents how hard the password is to brute-force. It is calculated as log2(charset_size ^ length). A password with 70+ bits of entropy is considered strong against modern attacks.

faq.question: Why exclude ambiguous characters?
faq.answer: Characters like 0 (zero), O (uppercase o), l (lowercase L), and I (uppercase i) look similar in many fonts. Excluding them prevents confusion when reading or manually typing passwords.
